title: Kaiming He - Wikipedia
excerpts: **Kaiming He** ( Chinese : 何恺明 ; pinyin : *Hé Kǎimíng* ) is a Chinese computer scientist who primarily researches computer vision and deep learning . <sup>[[ 1 ]]()</sup> He is an associate professor at Massachusetts Institute of Technology and works part-time as a Distinguished Scientist at Google DeepMind. <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> He is known as one of the creators of the residual neural network (ResNet) architecture. <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup>

title: Kaiming He（Fellow of the American National Academy of Artificial Intelligence, Associate Professor in the MIT Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, and recipient of the Future Science Prize.）_Baiduwiki
excerpts:
In March 2017, Kaiming He and his colleagues published Mask R-CNN, introducing a conceptually simple, flexible, and general framework for Object Instance Segmentation, capable of effectively detecting objects within images while simultaneously generating a high-quality segmentation mask for each instance.
1
Kaiming He (right) with his advisor, Professor Xiaoou Tang (left)
Talent Development
In the Spring of 2024, Kaiming He taught the course "Advances in Computer Vision" at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology.
29
Kaiming He teaching at MIT
Major Works
According to Google Scholar statistics, as of July 2023, Kaiming He has published 73 papers, with an H-index of 67 and over 460,000 citations.

title: IE PhD Alumnus Kaiming He ranked top five most highly cited scientist globally | Faculty of Engineering, CUHK
excerpts:
Section Title: IE PhD Alumnus Kaiming He ranked top five most highly cited scientist globally
Content:
2025-08-29
|Dr. Kaiming He, an alumnus of the IE PhD program in 2011, is an Associate Professor with tenure at MIT. His research areas include computer vision and deep learning. Dr. He was supervised by Prof. Sean Tang during his PhD study in Information Engineering at CUHK. Dr. He is best known for his work on Deep Residual Networks (ResNets), which is the most-cited paper of the twenty-first century, according to a Nature article. Currently, his total citation count is as high as 730,000, and the high citation count in the past five years is 610,000 in total. He Kaiming's most well - known research is ResNets, whose influence is not limited to the field of computer vision, and its design concept is widely applied in modern deep learning models.

Section Title: Title: Delving Deep into Rectifiers: Surpassing Human-Level Performance on ImageNet Classification
Content:
To our knowledge, our result is the first to surpass human-level performance (5.1%, Russakovsky et al.) on this visual recognition challenge.
